gram-to-cup_flour_ap.quick_explanation: In professional baking, weights are preferred over volumes because flour density varies significantly by how it is scooped. 120 grams is the standard weight for 1 US Cup of all-purpose flour.
gram-to-cup_flour_ap.real_world_example: If a recipe calls for '1 cup of flour' and you don't have a scale, spoon it into the cup until overflowing, then level it with a knife—roughly 120g.

faq_schema_qa.question: Why does my cup of flour weigh more than 120g?
faq_schema_qa.answer: If you 'dip and sweep' (scooping directly from the bag), the flour becomes compressed and can weigh up to 140g or more, potentially ruining your bake.

kilogram-to-pound.quick_explanation: The kilogram is the base unit of mass in the metric system (SI), while the pound is the primary unit in the US customary system. 1 kg is roughly the weight of a 1-liter bottle of water.
kilogram-to-pound.real_world_example: Checking a suitcase at the airport? A 23kg limit is approximately 50.7 lbs. Most airlines allow 50 lbs as a standard.

faq_schema_qa.question: Is 1 kg exactly 2.2 lbs?
faq_schema_qa.answer: No, it is approximately 2.20462 lbs. For rough mental math, doubling the kg and adding 10% is a very close shortcut.

celsius-to-fahrenheit.quick_explanation: Fahrenheit is primarily used in the US, while Celsius is the global standard. The scale offset is 32 degrees, meaning 0°C (freezing) is 32°F.
celsius-to-fahrenheit.real_world_example: A common 'comfortable' room temperature of 20°C corresponds to 68°F. A high fever is often defined as 38°C or 100.4°F.

faq_schema_qa.question: What temperature is the same in Celsius and Fahrenheit?
faq_schema_qa.answer: The scales cross at -40 degrees. So, -40°C is exactly equal to -40°F.
